meta.name: DAST-10 Drug Abuse Screening Test
meta.description: The DAST-10 is a brief, 10-item self-report screening instrument for identifying individuals who are abusing psychoactive drugs. It yields a quantitative index of the degree of consequences related to drug abuse.
meta.author: Skinner (1982)
meta.reference: Skinner HA. The Drug Abuse Screening Test. Addict Behav. 1982;7(4):363-371.
meta.instruction: The following questions concern information about your potential involvement with drugs, excluding alcohol and tobacco, during the past 12 months. Please answer Yes or No.

questions.text: Have you used drugs other than those required for medical reasons?
Do you abuse more than one drug at a time?
Are you always able to stop using drugs when you want to?
Have you ever had blackouts or flashbacks as a result of drug use?
Do you ever feel bad or guilty about your drug use?
Does your spouse (or parents) ever complain about your involvement with drugs?
Have you neglected your family because of your use of drugs?
Have you engaged in illegal activities in order to obtain drugs?
Have you ever experienced withdrawal symptoms (felt sick) when you stopped taking drugs?
Have you had medical problems as a result of your drug use (e.g., memory loss, hepatitis, convulsions, bleeding)?

ranges.label: No Problems Reported
ranges.description: Your responses do not indicate any drug-related problems. This indicates that your current substance-use-related risk is low and within a healthy range.
suggestions: Continue to maintain a drug-free lifestyle
Stay informed about the risks of drug use
Support friends or family members who may be struggling
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle
Stay informed about risks related to substance use

ranges.label: Low Level
ranges.description: Your responses suggest a low level of drug-related problems. Monitoring is recommended. A mild risk level suggests it may be helpful to pay attention to your usage patterns and maintain self-awareness.
suggestions: Be aware of your drug use patterns and their consequences
Consider discussing your drug use with a healthcare provider
Learn about healthy coping strategies for stress
Pay attention to your usage patterns and frequency
Learn about recommended safe-use limits

ranges.label: Moderate Level
ranges.description: Your responses suggest a moderate level of drug-related problems. Professional evaluation is recommended. Your score suggests that substance use may already be affecting certain areas of your life. Further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consider seeking a professional assessment for drug use
Explore counseling or therapy options
Identify triggers and develop alternative coping strategies
Talk to someone you trust about your concerns
Consider consulting an addiction medicine professional

ranges.label: Substantial Level
ranges.description: Your responses suggest a substantial level of drug-related problems. Intensive assessment and intervention are strongly recommended. Your score indicates elevated substance-use-related risk. Speaking with a professional for personalized support and guidance is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional treatment for drug use as soon as possible
Consider an intensive outpatient or inpatient treatment program
Inform trusted family members or friends for support
Avoid situations and people that trigger drug use
Seek professional addiction treatment as soon as possible

ranges.label: Severe Level
ranges.description: Your responses suggest a severe level of drug-related problems. Immediate professional intervention is necessary. Your score indicates elevated substance-use-related risk. Speaking with a professional for personalized support and guidance is recommended.
suggestions: Seek immediate professional help for drug dependence
Contact an addiction treatment center or hospital
Do not attempt to stop suddenly without medical supervision
Reach out to crisis hotlines if you are in distress
Seek professional addiction treatment as soon as possible
